summary: Final docs gate on plang-types after security v2 PASS + tester v5 PASS. New code carries strong XML docs already; gaps were user-facing (math.intdiv missing, code.load extension undocumented, divide semantics unclear), action teaching (intdiv.description.md missing), and architecture discoverability (no good_to_know.md entry pointing at the new typed-values shape). All filled. Applied architect's CLAUDE.md proposal (OBP Smell #7 — Courier reaches into Data.Value). Did NOT touch XML docs (already strong) and did NOT write new PLang .goal examples (tester's job, no gap on this branch).

changes.description: Applied architect v1 proposal: added item 7 to OBP Shape Smells checklist (Courier reaches into Data.Value), pointing to object_pattern_formal.md Rule #9.
Builder teaching for the new math.intdiv action — without it the LLM lacks the catalog hook.
Extended to make the 7/2 → 3.5 choice explicit and point to intdiv for truncating semantics.

Added intdiv section, divide promotion example, Number Policy section (Overflow/Precision axes, app.config vs per-action setting, power exponent cap). Fixed DivisionByZero → DivideByZero to match production error key.
Math row's action list updated to include intdiv.
code.load now documents three contribution kinds (ICode + [PlangType] + ITypeRenderer), the sealed-names guard with TypeLoadCollision, and the honest limit on what runtime registration can't rewrite.

Appended 'Typed values — app/types/<name>/, per-(type, format) renderers, type + kind as separate fields' entry. Covers folder contract, build-time Build(value) → kind vs action IClass.Build() distinction, per-(type, format) serializer dispatch, composition-not-union, runtime DLL loading with sealed names, OBP Rule #9 pointer.

findings.issue: New math.intdiv action shipped without a description.md — builder catalog hook missing.
findings.detail: Wrote one-line description explaining truncating-integer semantics, contrast with math.divide, pairing with math.modulo.

findings.issue: math.intdiv missing from user docs; divide's 7/2 → 3.5 behaviour not documented; Number Policy not documented at all (Overflow/Precision axes are user-visible).
findings.detail: Added intdiv section, divide example, full Number Policy section. Corrected error key DivisionByZero → DivideByZero to match production.

findings.issue: Math row missing intdiv in action list.
code.load extended to [PlangType] classes and ITypeRenderer implementations (Stage 7 of the branch), and the sealed-names guard added — none documented in user docs.
findings.detail: Added three-contribution-kinds explanation, sealed-names list with TypeLoadCollision, honest limit on compiled slots / shipped .pr stamps.

findings.issue: No discoverability hook for the new typed-values shape (app/types/<name>/ folder contract, type+kind separation, per-(type, format) renderer dispatch).
findings.detail: Appended a typed-values entry that covers the folder contract, the two Builds (action vs type), the renderer dispatch shape, composition-not-union, runtime DLL loading + sealed names, and the OBP Rule #9 pointer. The architect plan remains the canonical design narrative; this is the discoverability layer.

findings.issue: OBP Smell Checklist did not yet include the 'courier reaches into Data.Value' smell that architect Rule #9 codifies.
findings.detail: Applied architect's proposal verbatim — item 7 added with grep detection pattern and pointer to object_pattern_formal.md Rule #9.

findings.issue: Security v2 F4: math.round with Decimals outside [0,28] (decimal) or [0,15] (double) throws ArgumentOutOfRangeException uncaught by Wrap. Not blocking per security verdict but a known gap.
findings.detail: Did NOT document a Round-clamps-Decimals behaviour in math.md because the clamp doesn't exist in code yet. When a future coder lands the clamp, the math.md Number Policy section is the natural home.

claude_md_proposals.reason: Material rule addition that applies to all future runtime2 work: the OBP Smell Checklist needs the 'courier reaches into Data.Value' entry to catch mid-pipeline data.Value as X / is X switches without bots having to know canonical Rule #9 by heart. Points to object_pattern_formal.md, doesn't duplicate.
